title: kyllinga umbellata
intro: Kyllinga umbellata, also known as Water Wriggle-grass or Finger Grass, is a perennial aquatic plant that grows in wetlands and is native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, and its medicinal properties include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Kyllinga umbellata include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, Kyllinga umbellata is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
